methods.description: This method interprets excessive Terrestrial Cosmogenic Nuclide (TCN) ages in alluvial environments, considering the possibility of inheritance during transport rather than in situ surface processes.

steps.description: Analyze TCN ages of alluvial-fan deposits that are less than 75 years old, which show ages of 2-71 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TCN ages of alluvial-fan deposits
inputs: TCN age data of alluvial-fan deposits

steps.description: Compare TCN ages with expected ages to determine if inheritance during transport is a factor.
steps.searchable_summary: compare TCN ages with expected ages

methods.description: This method interprets Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) ages within a stratigraphic framework to determine consistent depositional ages.

steps.description: Analyze OSL ages to determine consistency with the local stratigraphic framework.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze OSL ages for stratigraphic consistency

steps.description: Interpret progressively older OSL ages with depth as evidence of consistent results.
Consider alternate interpretations of OSL ages, such as eluviation of fine particles affecting age results.
steps.searchable_summary: consider alternate interpretations of OSL ages
